title: Beat out by a hot tamale: Maine hosts the second-best specialty food festival in America, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Maine Whoopie Pie Festival in Dover-Foxcroft ranked No. 2 in USA Today's 2026 top 10 specialty food festivals.
The festival on June 13 features bakers with classic and unique whoopie pie flavors, along with contests and entertainment.
The Delta Hot Tamale Festival in Mississippi secured the No. 1 spot in USA Today's 10Best Readers' Choice Awards.

title: Man accused of recording people in South Shore library bathroom
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Thomas Campbell, 63, of Marshfield, arrested for allegedly recording individuals in Cohasset library restroom.
Hidden recording device found in Paul Pratt Public Library; forensic analysis confirmed illegal recordings.
Campbell charged with illegal wiretapping, held on $2,500 bail, arraignment set for Friday in Quincy District Court.

title: Energy assistance runs out amid spiking oil prices
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Heating oil prices surge 30% due to the war in Iran, increasing costs for Massachusetts households.
Over 70% of ABCD’s 170,000 fuel assistance clients exhausted their benefits amid prolonged cold weather.
Healey administration raised fuel assistance maximum to $1,400, but funds might still be insufficient amidst rising costs.

title: Investing in quality: The benefits of well-made furniture and art
author: Regina Cole
bullets: Designer Kate Maloney emphasizes using locally made, quality furniture that can move between homes in Cambridge and Somerville.
Massachusetts-based Tracy Glover Studio crafted custom glass fixtures that inspired the color scheme of the Cambridge kitchen.
Designs in Blinds & Drapes resized draperies, highlighting the advantage of repurposing well-made, high-quality home furnishings.

title: ‘He drives them’: New Bruins forward Lukas Reichel made an instant impact at TD Garden
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Lukas Reichel scored a goal and an assist in his Bruins debut, contributing to a 6-1 win over the Winnipeg Jets.
The Bruins acquired Reichel from the Vancouver Canucks for a 2026 sixth-round pick before the NHL trade deadline.
Reichel's performance helped propel the Bruins as they compete in a tight race for playoff positions in the Eastern Conference.

title: Ask the Remodeler: How to keep melted snow out of your basement
author: Mark Philben -- Globe Correspondent
bullets: Shovel snow from basement window wells and use stone beds to prevent leaks during snow melts.
Ensure gutters and downspouts are clear to direct water away from foundations and avoid basement flooding.
Consider long-term solutions like French drains or sump pumps with battery backups for high water table areas.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a quiz to test knowledge of the latest Greater Boston news stories.
Peter Chianca has been Boston.com's general assignment editor since 2019.
Chianca has extensive experience as a news editor, columnist, and music writer in Greater Boston.

title: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers vote to authorize strike as contract negotiations stall
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers voted to authorize a strike after contract negotiations stalled since voluntary union recognition in June 2024.
Key negotiation issues include job protections and the impact of self-service kiosks on part-time and seasonal workers.
The union, affiliated with United Autoworkers Local 1596, received over 80% support for strike authorization but has no immediate strike plans.

title: Alex Cora believes this Red Sox player will slug 30 home runs this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora predicts outfielder Wilyer Abreu will hit 30 home runs in the 2026 season.
FanGraphs' 2026 projections show no Red Sox player hitting 20 or more home runs, with Abreu projected at 18.
Abreu hit 22 homers in 115 games last season and showcased power during the 2026 World Baseball Classic.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots taking ‘fascinating’ offensive tackle with pick No. 31
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots may select Arizona State offensive tackle Max Iheanachor with the 31st overall pick in the 2026 NFL Draft.
Iheanachor, a former East Los Angeles Community College player, is noted for his athleticism and potential by NFL experts.
The Patriots seek to find a long-term replacement for veteran tackle Morgan Moses, who turned 35 this month.
